summary.notFoundBehavior: SPA with Vite dev server — serves identical index.html for both / and /nonexistent. React crashes on load (useMemo not imported), so BOTH routes render a blank screen.

criticalBug.description: useMemo is used at App.tsx:1933 but is NOT in the React import at line 1. Import only includes: useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback.
criticalBug.effect: ReferenceError: useMemo is not defined — crashes the entire <App> component on every page load. React 19 internal warning fires: 'An error occurred in the <App> component. Consider adding an error boundary.'
criticalBug.confirmed_by: Playwright headless browser PAGEERROR: useMemo is not defined
criticalBug.fix: Add useMemo to the React import: import React, { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback, useMemo } from 'react';

notFoundPage.behavior: Vite dev server serves index.html for all routes (SPA fallback). No React Router configured (hasSPARouter: false per page-map). Since the app crashes due to useMemo error, /nonexistent also shows a blank screen — same as /.

consoleIssues.message: An error occurred in the <App> component. Consider adding an error boundary to your tree to customize error handling behavior. Visit https://react.dev/link/error-boundaries to learn more about error boundaries.
consoleIssues.note: React 19 internal warning triggered by the useMemo crash

errorBoundary.note: No ErrorBoundary class component (componentDidCatch) found anywhere in src/. No error.tsx (Vite SPA, not Next.js). Because there is no ErrorBoundary, the useMemo crash causes the entire app to render as a blank white screen with no user-facing error message or recovery UI.
errorBoundary.recommendation: Wrap <App /> in main.tsx with an ErrorBoundary component to show a recovery message instead of a blank screen

inputValidation.reason: Could not test live — app crashes on load due to useMemo ReferenceError before any form is rendered

staticAnalysis.portAlphaInput: HTML number type blocks non-numeric at browser level, but addPort() uses parseInt(port) with no NaN guard — if port is somehow non-numeric, parseInt returns NaN which gets stored as the port value
staticAnalysis.portRangeValidation: MISSING — addPort() has no check for valid port range (1-65535). Port 99999 or 0 would be accepted.
staticAnalysis.nameLengthValidation: MISSING — no maxLength attribute on name input, no length check in addPort(). 500-char names accepted.
staticAnalysis.nameRequired: addPort() has if(name) guard — empty name is blocked. Port is optional.

externalLinks.note: App could not render due to crash. Static source analysis shows no hardcoded external hrefs in App.tsx or PortalManager.tsx. All links (deployUrl, githubUrl, localhost:port) are user-provided dynamic data.

offlineBanner.style: fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 bg-amber-500/90 text-black text-sm px-4 py-2
offlineBanner.message: API 서버에 연결할 수 없습니다 — bun run start로 서버를 시작하세요
offlineBanner.tauri_guard: Hidden in Tauri mode (isTauri() returns true)
offlineBanner.note: Implementation is correct and well-designed. Currently hidden because API server is online.

tests.name: nonexistent-route-404
tests.detail: Blank page on /nonexistent — Vite serves index.html but app crashes (useMemo not defined), rendering blank screen. No custom 404 UI exists.

tests.name: console-errors-main-page
tests.detail: PAGEERROR: useMemo is not defined — crashes <App> component on every load

tests.name: console-warnings-main-page
tests.detail: React 19 warning: 'An error occurred in the <App> component. Consider adding an error boundary.' — triggered by useMemo crash

tests.name: offline-amber-banner-hidden-when-online
tests.detail: Amber offline banner correctly absent when API server is reachable (confirmed via source: condition is apiServerOnline===false)

tests.name: offline-banner-implementation
tests.detail: Offline amber banner fully implemented at App.tsx:1970-1973. Polls /api/ports every 30s with 2s timeout. Retry button present. Web-mode only (isTauri() guard).

tests.name: react-error-boundary
tests.detail: No React ErrorBoundary found in src/. The current useMemo crash proves this gap — blank white screen with no recovery path. React 19 itself warns about this.

tests.name: invalid-port-abc
tests.detail: Could not test live — app crashes before rendering. Static analysis: addPort() uses parseInt(port) without NaN guard. HTML number input blocks 'abc' at browser level but no range validation.

tests.name: invalid-port-99999
tests.detail: Could not test live — app crashes before rendering. Static analysis: no port range validation in addPort(). Values outside 1-65535 would be accepted.

tests.name: invalid-port-no-crash
tests.detail: Could not test live — app crashes before rendering due to useMemo error

tests.name: long-name-500-chars
tests.detail: Could not test live — app crashes before rendering. Static analysis: no maxLength on name input, no length guard in addPort().

tests.name: broken-links-check
tests.detail: No hardcoded external links in App.tsx or PortalManager.tsx. All href links (deployUrl, githubUrl, localhost:port) are dynamic user-provided data.

tests.name: amber-banner-implementation-quality
tests.detail: Amber banner design is correct and complete: fixed positioning, 30s health poll with 2s timeout guard, retry button, isTauri() guard. Well implemented.

issues.issue: useMemo not imported — ReferenceError crashes <App> on every page load, rendering a blank screen
issues.fix: Change line 1: import React, { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback, useMemo } from 'react';

issues.issue: No React ErrorBoundary — any uncaught JS error (like the current useMemo crash) produces a blank white screen with zero user feedback or recovery option
issues.fix: Add an ErrorBoundary wrapper in main.tsx around <App />

issues.issue: No port range validation in addPort() — out-of-range values (0, 99999, negative) are silently accepted
issues.fix: Add: const portNum = parseInt(port); if (port && (isNaN(portNum) || portNum < 1 || portNum > 65535)) { showToast('포트는 1-65535 범위여야 합니다', 'error'); return; }

issues.issue: No maxLength on project name/description fields — 500+ character names are accepted and stored
issues.fix: Add maxLength={100} to name input, maxLength={500} to description input

issues.issue: No custom 404 page — Vite serves index.html for unknown paths. When app works, /nonexistent shows the full app rather than a 404 message
issues.fix: Add a catch-all check on mount or configure Vite to return a 404 status for unknown paths
